studies.title: Multi-center retrospective and prospective data analysis with additive app-based therapy to optimize amblyopia therapy
studies.brief_summary: Amblyopia is one of the most common childhood vision disorders. Since orientation and visual perception can be severely hampered, the visual impression of the worse eye is suppressed by the brain in the developmental phase of vision. This leads to an underdevelopment of the cortical visual centers, the eye becomes amblyope. As therapy there is currently only the possibility of occlusion therapy of the good eye (after binocular eyeglass correction). The aim of the study is to optimize the amblyopie therapy by evaluating the course of treatment with the use of the Caterna visual therapy, which can be carried out online and which is used in addition to the occlusion therapy. This is intended to provide improved therapeutic success in amblyopic patients through active stimulation. The Caterna Vision Therapy Games are played on the computer for 90 days daily for 30-45 minutes during occlusion.
